eventName: MV Hondius Andes virus response and U.S. monitoring cluster

locations.name: Port of Granadilla, Tenerife
locations.description: Cruise ship arrived in Tenerife for response coordination, disembarkation, and repatriation.

locations.name: Ushuaia cruise departure context
locations.description: Southern Cone cruise-route context for the MV Hondius outbreak.

locations.name: UNMC/Nebraska Medicine National Quarantine Unit
locations.description: U.S. repatriation, quarantine, and treatment location for passengers linked to MV Hondius.

locations.name: Emory University Hospital
locations.description: Supplemental/local reports describe MV Hondius passengers monitored or treated in Atlanta.

locations.name: New Jersey resident monitoring
locations.description: State health department update for exposed New Jersey residents under monitoring after MV Hondius travel.

locations.name: North Carolina passenger monitoring
locations.description: North Carolina health officials reported one passenger evacuated to Nebraska for quarantine with no current North Carolina cases.

locations.name: New York resident reports
locations.description: Supplemental news reports described New York residents linked to the MV Hondius response; retained as supplemental until matched to a public-health source.

locations.name: RIVM / GGD monitoring area
locations.description: Dutch public-health monitoring and guidance related to MV Hondius passengers and contacts.

locations.name: France passenger monitoring
locations.description: Supplemental reports describe French passenger monitoring and one serious case after repatriation.

locations.name: Madrid military hospital
locations.description: Location-specific evidence reports one Spanish passenger in quarantine at a Madrid military hospital who tested positive.

locations.name: Bichat Hospital, Paris
locations.description: Location-specific evidence reports one French passenger treated in intensive care at Bichat Hospital.

locations.name: Eindhoven repatriation quarantine
locations.description: AP reports repatriation flights arrived in Eindhoven and passengers or crew were placed into quarantine; no case count is assigned to this marker.

locations.name: Radboud University Medical Center, Nijmegen
locations.description: Location-specific AP and CIDRAP evidence reports a patient treated at Radboud UMC and 12 hospital staff placed in preventive quarantine.

locations.name: Argentina exposure investigation
locations.description: AP and CIDRAP report Argentina planned an exposure-source investigation around locations visited before boarding; no deaths are assigned to this investigation marker.

locations.name: Rotterdam ship cleaning route
locations.description: AP reports the MV Hondius was sailing back toward Rotterdam for cleaning and disinfection; no case count is assigned to this marker.

locations.name: Victoria, British Columbia hospital assessment
locations.description: Canada-linked MV Hondius passenger hospital assessment/testing location. Official Canadian framing is presumptive positive with national-lab confirmation pending; this is not a U.S. confirmed case and no deaths are assigned to this marker.
locations.countNotes: Location-specific count: one presumptive-positive symptomatic passenger in hospital assessment/testing in Victoria. Confirmation was pending at Canada.ca/PHAC publication.

locations.name: Vancouver Island isolation and monitoring
locations.description: Canadian MV Hondius passengers were transferred to dedicated accommodations on Vancouver Island for isolation and daily public-health monitoring. Monitoring/quarantine does not mean confirmed infection.
sourceNames: British Columbia Office of the Provincial Health Officer
locations.countNotes: Location-specific monitoring/isolation count for the four Canadians returned to B.C.; this should not be read as four confirmed infections.

locations.name: Yukon passenger origin context
locations.description: Supplemental reports identify the presumptive-positive Canadian passenger as being from Yukon. This origin marker carries no separate people count to avoid double-counting the Victoria testing location.
locations.countNotes: Origin context only; no separate confirmed/death count is assigned to Yukon.

locations.name: United States exposure monitoring area
locations.description: Supplemental reporting attributed to CDC described 41 people in the United States under monitoring for possible exposure, while reporting no known U.S. cases. This marker is monitoring/exposure response only.
locations.countNotes: U.S.-scope monitoring count; not a confirmed U.S. Andes-virus case count.

countSnapshots.sourceName: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
countSnapshots.notes: Latest official ECDC daily count used for the canonical official event count.

countSnapshots.sourceName: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
countSnapshots.notes: CDC current situation page states no Andes virus cases have been confirmed in the United States as a result of this outbreak to date; this is a U.S.-scope count note, not a replacement for ECDC's global outbreak count.
Earlier WHO Disease Outbreak News count retained in history.

RIVM reported three symptomatic people in the Netherlands tested after aircraft contact; two results were negative and one was pending at publication. This is monitoring evidence, not a confirmed Netherlands case count.
Health-system update describing U.S. citizens at UNMC/Nebraska Medicine quarantine and treatment units.
State health department monitoring update; these are exposed residents, not confirmed New Jersey cases.
Supplemental local news report around Emory monitoring; not an official confirmed case count.
AP-syndicated passenger tracing report; supplemental evidence and not an official U.S. case count.
CBS Atlanta reported two people evaluated at Emory, including one symptomatic passenger who tested negative for Andes virus according to HHS; supplemental evidence only.
Supplemental report describing one French and one American positive passenger; not used as official count unless corroborated.
Reuters-syndicated evacuation report; used as supplemental travel/response evidence, not a case count.
Counts vary by source/update. CIDRAP reports the outbreak grew to 11 total cases, 9 lab-confirmed, and 3 deaths. Location-specific quarantine counts apply only to Radboud/Nijmegen hospital personnel.

Counts vary by source/update. AP correction states WHO says 9 confirmed and 2 suspected worldwide; AP remains supplemental_news unless corroborated by WHO/ECDC/CDC. Evacuated passengers and crew are metadata, not cases.

PHAC reported one of four high-risk people in quarantine and symptom monitoring was presumptively positive for Andes hantavirus, with national-lab confirmation pending; the symptomatic person, their spouse, and a third person were moved to hospital for assessment. This is Canada response evidence, not an event-wide global confirmed-count update.

countSnapshots.sourceName: British Columbia Office of the Provincial Health Officer
countSnapshots.notes: B.C. stated four Canadians arriving from MV Hondius would be screened, assessed, isolated for at least 21 days, and monitored daily by public-health teams.

NBC described a Canadian passenger hospitalized in Victoria after a positive/presumptive test following MV Hondius exposure. Hantamap keeps this as supplemental evidence and preserves the official pending-confirmation framing.

NBC-style reporting said CDC was monitoring at least 41 people across 16 U.S. states with no known U.S. cases; this is monitoring/exposure-response evidence, not a confirmed U.S. Andes-virus case count.

summary: Canonical event cluster for the 2026 MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak and related public-health monitoring, quarantine, treatment, testing, and local-health follow-up. Counts are not summed across articles. Location markers show location-specific counts; event-wide death counts stay in event-wide sections only.

locations.name: Quad-County region
locations.description: Local public-health advisory region for a confirmed hantavirus case.

countSnapshots.sourceName: Carson City Health and Human Services via Carson Now
countSnapshots.notes: Local health officials confirmed a single hantavirus case in the Quad-County region.
Supplemental local news report referencing Quad-County officials.

summary: Local public-health-backed Nevada event for a single confirmed hantavirus case in the Quad-County region. This is unrelated to the MV Hondius Andes virus cluster.

locations.name: United States NNDSS surveillance area
locations.description: CDC weekly notifiable disease surveillance context for Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome.

countSnapshots.notes: Official surveillance source is tracked, but Hantamap does not infer a case count until a table extraction is verified.

summary: Official U.S. surveillance context for 2026 Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome. Counts remain null until table extraction is verified to avoid overstating surveillance data.
